failure_modes.name: Confident Out-of-Scope Claim
failure_modes.definition: Bot asserts that eBay Live does NOT support or have something, for a topic not covered in the knowledge base, instead of saying 'I don't know.'
failure_modes.pass_criteria: Bot says it doesn't have information about that topic and directs the user to ebay.com/help or the eBay Live FAQ.
failure_modes.fail_criteria: Bot states a definitive 'eBay Live does not support X' or 'X is not available' for topics not addressed in the KB.
example_quotes: Using a pre-recorded video is not currently supported for eBay Live.
eBay Live does not support scheduling.
Digital products are not supported for live sale.
failure_modes.mechanism: Bot extrapolates absence of KB coverage into a negative policy assertion.
failure_modes.downstream_risk: Users abandon valid use cases based on invented restrictions.

failure_modes.name: Invented Troubleshooting Steps
failure_modes.definition: Bot provides specific technical troubleshooting advice — steps, settings, workarounds, or connectivity guidance — that is not present in the knowledge base.
failure_modes.pass_criteria: Bot acknowledges it doesn't have troubleshooting guidance for this issue and directs the user to eBay support or the Help Center.
failure_modes.fail_criteria: Bot gives specific technical steps (e.g., clear cache, restart app, avoid VPNs, adjust encoder settings) that are not grounded in the KB.
example_quotes: Try restarting the app or browser and clearing cache.
Avoid using cell signal boosters or VPNs during your live session.
Set your encoder bitrate to at least 2500 kbps for stable streaming.
failure_modes.mechanism: Bot invents plausible-sounding technical depth for troubleshooting scenarios not covered in the KB.
failure_modes.downstream_risk: Users waste time on unverified steps; may conclude their real problem is unsolvable after invented steps fail.

failure_modes.name: Invented Process Details
failure_modes.definition: Bot describes specific workflows, URLs, feature capabilities, or step-by-step processes that are not present in the knowledge base.
failure_modes.pass_criteria: Bot describes only what is explicitly stated in the KB; for anything beyond that, it says it doesn't have that information.
failure_modes.fail_criteria: Bot invents specific URLs, describes features that don't exist or aren't documented, or outlines workflows not grounded in the KB.
example_quotes: Submit the interest form at ebay.com/live.
You can schedule your livestream in advance from the seller hub.
Use the multi-seller collaboration option in your eBay Live dashboard.
failure_modes.mechanism: Bot invents positive workflow details that sound plausible but have no KB basis.
failure_modes.downstream_risk: Users follow non-existent workflows; waste time searching for URLs and features that don't exist.
failure_modes.note: Distinct from FM1: FM1 is negative assertions ('not supported'), FM3 is positive assertions ('here is how to do X') where X doesn't exist in the KB.

failure_modes.name: Failure to Escalate on Truly Unknown Topics
failure_modes.definition: Bot gives a confident, substantive answer to a question that is genuinely outside its knowledge base and domain, instead of saying 'I don't know' and referring the user elsewhere.
failure_modes.pass_criteria: Bot recognizes the question is outside its domain, says it doesn't have that information, and directs the user to ebay.com/help or a more appropriate resource.
failure_modes.fail_criteria: Bot addresses an out-of-scope topic with confident framing or invents details for systems and topics not covered in the KB.
example_quotes: eBay Live is best used as a complementary sales channel alongside your existing inventory management workflow.
You can integrate third-party analytics with eBay Live using the standard API endpoints.
For tax purposes, eBay Live sales are treated the same as regular eBay transactions.
failure_modes.mechanism: Bot fails to recognize topic as out-of-scope and applies confident framing to fill the response gap.
failure_modes.downstream_risk: Users trust bot framing as authoritative for domains the bot knows nothing about; leads to bad decisions.
failure_modes.note: Distinct from FM2: FM2 is inventing technical depth within an in-scope topic; FM4 is drifting to a different domain (inventory management, tax, third-party integrations) without escalating.

failure_modes.name: Potentially Misleading Inference
failure_modes.definition: Bot makes a plausible but unverified inference from KB content, stating it as established fact, in a way that could mislead users.
failure_modes.pass_criteria: Bot clearly signals uncertainty when stating anything not directly grounded in the KB, or avoids making the inference entirely.
failure_modes.fail_criteria: Bot states a reasonable-sounding inference as authoritative guidance without any hedging language.
example_quotes: Avoid placing duplicate bids for the same amount.
eBay Live focuses on livestream video shopping through standard devices.
Sellers must have at least 100 feedback ratings to qualify for eBay Live.
failure_modes.mechanism: Bot applies reasonable inference from adjacent KB content but states it as fact rather than as inference.
failure_modes.downstream_risk: Plausible-sounding inferences are harder for users to question than obvious hallucinations; may be wrong in specific edge cases.
failure_modes.note: Distinct from FM1/FM3: FM5 involves overstating confidence about inferences that might be reasonable but are undocumented. FM1/FM3 involve inventing things with no KB basis at all.

annotation_protocol.scoring: Binary per failure mode: 1 = present, 0 = absent
annotation_protocol.independence: Score each failure mode independently; a trace can have multiple failure modes
checklist: FM1: Does the response contain a statement of form 'eBay Live does not support X' where X is KB-absent?
FM2: Does the response give specific technical troubleshooting steps not grounded in the KB?
FM3: Does the response describe a specific URL, workflow, or feature not in the KB?
FM4: Is the topic genuinely outside eBay Live, and did the bot answer confidently instead of deferring?
FM5: Does the response state an inference as fact where the KB does not directly support the claim, without hedging?
